backend/execution/models.py:102 DEMO_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS (unchanged; used only in views.py safety_rails display)
REPLACED by the registry: signal_proposals.py:186 (E1a), signal_planning.py:229 (E1b), signal_promotion.py:137 (E2a/E3)
NOT CHANGED (Windows bridge, separate deploy — follow-up): scripts/mt5_signal_bridge.py:79/84/298/792/926 ALLOWED_SYMBOLS/DEMO_ORDER_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS; scripts/mt5_demo_bridge.py:73/312

expected_results: A BrokerInstrument-backed registry replaces the hardcoded allowlist at the 3 Wayond gates; a symbol is accepted iff resolvable to a broker symbol on the account, else fail-closed with SYMBOL_NOT_AVAILABLE_ON_BROKER / SYMBOL_MAPPING_AMBIGUOUS / SYMBOL_REGISTRY_UNAVAILABLE.
EURUSD/GBPUSD/XAUUSD still accepted (default baseline for accounts with no synced cache) — existing E2a/E3/auto-shadow tests unchanged.

BTCUSD accepted when the broker registry supports it (exact) or maps unambiguously (suffix); rejected fail-closed otherwise; ambiguous suffix mapping fails closed; registry unavailable (strict + no cache) fails closed.
The provider (Wayond) symbol is preserved (payload['provider_symbol']) and the resolved BROKER symbol is used for the order (payload['symbol']).
The registry is read-only (no order_send, no network); the network fetch is isolated to the sync command; migration additive; full suite green.
actual_results: execution.tests_broker_symbols: Ran 16 tests OK — (1) EURUSD baseline accepted; (2) XAUUSD baseline accepted; (3) BTCUSD accepted (exact) when broker cache has it (+ metadata); (4) BTCUSD rejected SYMBOL_NOT_AVAILABLE_ON_BROKER when cache lacks it (cache authoritative, EURUSD still accepted from cache); (5) suffix BTCUSD. mapped unambiguously; (6) BTCUSD.+BTCUSD+ -> SYMBOL_MAPPING_AMBIGUOUS; (7) strict + no cache -> SYMBOL_REGISTRY_UNAVAILABLE; (8) provider preserved + exact wins over suffix (not ambiguous); disabled instrument ignored; blank -> invalid_symbol; (9) demo promotion payload symbol=BTCUSD. + provider_symbol=BTCUSD; (11) EURUSD demo + (11b) EURUSD shadow unchanged; (12) BTCUSD trades when broker supports it; (10) AST boundary: broker_symbols references no order_send/order_check/mt5/requests/urllib/socket; sync upsert enables/disables (stale->disabled, not deleted).
E1a/E1b/E2a/E3 (updated reject codes): Ran 102 tests OK (BTCUSD/GBPJPY now reject SYMBOL_NOT_AVAILABLE_ON_BROKER via the default baseline).
Full backend suite: Ran 509 tests OK.
makemigrations --check execution: No changes detected (0015 captures the model). make governance-check OK; secret-scan OK; EVIDENCE-LINT PASS.
Adversarial 4-lens review (fail-closed / broker-symbol-and-audit / backward-compat / boundary-and-sync) + opus synthesis: see reviewer / PR.
limitations: Repo-only. NOT deployed. No order_send, no arming, no risk-cap change. The currently-armed demo runs on the deployed (baseline) code and is unaffected by this branch. E3 remains RED.

The Windows MT5 bridge (scripts/mt5_signal_bridge.py) still enforces its own hardcoded ALLOWED_SYMBOLS/DEMO_ORDER_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS. A non-baseline symbol (e.g. BTCUSD) would still be rejected AT THE BRIDGE until that is replaced with mt5.symbol_info validation + acceptance of the backend-resolved broker_symbol — a separate Windows-deploy packet. So this packet makes the BACKEND broker-aware; end-to-end BTCUSD execution also needs the bridge change + a broker-symbol sync.

The BrokerInstrument cache is empty until populated by `sync_broker_instruments` (which fetches the bridge GET /mt5/symbols). Until then every account uses the DEFAULT baseline (EURUSD/GBPUSD/XAUUSD) — i.e. no behavioural change without a sync + deploy. Populating prod is a deploy/preflight step, out of scope here.
The strategy path (strategies/signal_engine.py) and the DEMO_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS display constant were intentionally left unchanged (separate concerns from the Wayond signal path).
reviewer: Adversarial 4-lens Workflow (fail-closed / broker-symbol-and-audit / backward-compat / boundary-and-sync) + opus synthesis: decision GO, zero must-fix. Traced every hard requirement to file:line and confirmed: no symbol trades unverified against the account's broker; provider symbol preserved (payload.provider_symbol) while the resolved broker_symbol is placed (payload.symbol); baseline EURUSD/GBPUSD/XAUUSD unchanged for un-synced accounts; suffix mapping only when unambiguous; read path imports no order_send/mt5/network (AST test_10). Non-blocking nits (all left as-is): per-call os.getenv for BROKER_SYMBOL_REGISTRY_STRICT; the separate strategies/signal_engine.py STRATEGY path still uses hardcoded SIGNAL_ALLOWED_SYMBOLS (out of scope); the broad except Exception on the ORM query fails closed to SYMBOL_REGISTRY_UNAVAILABLE by design.
